note: Ji Hoon Park, Jiahao Chen, and Mateo Ishikawa are clustered in the park at night.

Mateo has a thing for the silence of the water over the noise of the city.
Mateo, Ji-Hoon, and Jiahao are the only ones breaking the silence by the water tonight.
Mateo views silence as a baseline of efficiency rather than a void.
Ji Hoon Park carries barley tea in a hot thermos for his circulation.
Ari Rosenbaum finds silence to be interference; a perspective worth noting next time the river feels too quiet.

Ari Rosenbaum understands that cooling physics cannot be rushed.
Mateo advocates for thermal stabilization to prevent internal condensation on delicate parts.

Mateo views the environment as a series of microscopic tolerances under threat by salt.
